name: Unwilling Host
short_description: This is a first-person indie horror game with VHS effects. Be careful—you're facing a terrifying entity.
detailed_description:
This is the first game I've ever made! I'm so happy to have completed it!

Game Background:
Ella is critically ill and near death. Her loving parents performed a sacrificial ritual to an unknown entity in a desperate attempt to save her—but instead invited a deadly parasite into their daughter. After the ritual, Ella’s consciousness was torn apart: one half became controlled malice, the other a remnant of survival instinct. You are that neutral fragment of her mind—a scale destined to tip toward evil.

Gameplay:
This horror game is a first-person walking simulator featuring VHS effects and a chilling atmosphere. You’ll explore environments, confront anomalies, and unlock new areas. Along the way, many eerie and terrifying events await. Be careful!

The full playthrough lasts approximately 30 minutes.

Key Features:

- Walking Simulator

- VHS Filter

- Terrifying Atmosphere

supported_languages: English, French, German, Spanish - Spain, Russian, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, Portuguese - Brazil, Korean, Italian, Turkish, Polish

reviews.review_text:
Short indie horror game set in a dark mansion, focused on atmosphere, simple exploration, a couple of jump scares, and one puzzle.

The beginning follows a familiar indie horror setup, with your unnamed character standing at the entrance of a mansion. It is immediately clear that something terrible has happened inside. Bloodstains lead down the stairs, and there is a pool of blood right beneath your feet. None of the light switches work, leaving the space almost completely dark, except for a single table lamp near the door that seems to run on its own power source or batteries. In practice, this simply guides you to follow the light.

Nothing of interest is explicitly marked. You can only interact with objects by moving the small circle in the center of the screen over a hotspot. When the circle turns into a white dot, you can press the interaction button. This mostly applies to doors, light switches, and keys that unlock the next area. Keys can be easy to miss, although they are usually placed in obvious locations such as desks or sinks.

There are many other objects scattered throughout the house, but they serve only to build atmosphere. That atmosphere, along with the game’s linear direction, is one of its strengths. The visuals contribute significantly, with the screen locked in a 4:3 aspect ratio and covered in a grainy filter that makes it feel as if you are watching through an old camera.

Gameplay focuses on following the intended path and observing events. These include familiar horror elements such as doors locking or opening behind you, lights suddenly going out, and sharp, loud noises. The experience revolves around a little girl who appears unexpectedly around corners or behind you, as well as a strange doll.

There is only one short chase sequence and a single number puzzle to vary the pacing, but the entire experience is very brief and can be completed in under half an hour. After the house, you move into a short basement section where you have a light source, followed by a final scene that lasts a few minutes and emphasizes visuals over interaction.

Just beat the game, Its a super trippy and scary realistic horror game where you explore a giant bloody haunted house encountering haunted dolls with glow up purple eyes, little ghost girls and shadow ghosts in spacey worlds. Super fun and cool game highly reccomended to play while smoking the kush
